markdown:
#  What granular permissions are available for the project's Budget tool? 
##  Background 
In Procore, the term granular permissions refers to task-based privileges that can be granted to users with 'Read Only' or 'Standard' level permissions on certain tools through the users' assigned Permissions Template. 
The table below shows the granular permissions that are available for the project's Budget tool.
##  Answer   
| Create, Edit, and Delete Budget Changes  | Grants users the privilege to create new Budget Changes, and edit or delete existing Budget Changes. See [Create Budget Changes](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/create-budget-changes).  |  
| --- | --- |  
| Delete Budget Line Items  | Grants users the privilege to delete budget line items. See [Delete a Budget Line Item](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/delete-a-budget-line-item).  |  
| Import line items From CSV  | Along with the 'Create and Edit Original Budget Amount' granular permission, this granular permission grants users the privilege to import a budget from a file. See [Import a Budget](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/import-a-budget).  |  
| Lock Budget  | Grants users the privilege to lock the Budget tool. See [Lock a Budget](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/lock-a-budget).  |  
| Modify original budget amount  | Grants users the privilege to create and edit the original budget amount. See [Add a Budget Line Item](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/add-a-budget-line-item) and [Edit a Budget](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/edit-a-budget).  |  
| Send and retrieve from ERP  | Grants users the privilege to send data from the Budget tool to the ERP Integrations tool and also to retrieve information from the ERP Integrations tool before it is accepted for export to an integrated ERP system by an accounting approver. For more information, see [Send a Budget to ERP Integrations for Accounting Acceptance](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/send-a-budget-to-erp-integrations-for-accounting-acceptance) and [Retrieve a Budget from ERP Integrations Before Acceptance](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/retrieve-a-budget-from-erp-integrations-before-acceptance).  |  
| View Direct Cost Details  | 
  * Grants users the privilege to view transaction-level details being populated from the Direct Cost tool via a popup window that appears in the Budget tool. The Direct Costs columns is visible when creating, editing, and viewing line items. See [Read a Budget](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/budget-project/tutorials/read-a-budget).
  * This granular permission provides users with the ability to click a value in the 'Direct Costs' column of a budget view to open a popup window that shows direct cost transaction detail.
  * If your company is using either the Procore + Sage 300 CRE® integration or the Vista® Integration by Procore, this setting must be enabled in the permissions template that is assigned to Procore end users performing budget exports.

 |  
## See Also
  * [Which Procore tools support granular permissions?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-procore-tools-support-granular-permissions)
  * [What granular permissions are available for the Project level Admin tool?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-granular-permissions-are-available-for-the-project-level-admin-tool)
  * [What granular permissions are available for Project level Workflows? (Beta)](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-granular-permissions-are-available-for-project-level-workflows)


In Procore, a _Permissions Template_ is a set of _role-based permissions_ that can be assigned to one or more Procore users. The permission settings in a template define what actions users can perform and what information they can view in each Procore tool
title: What granular permissions are available for the project's Budget tool?
